name: English Cheese Pie
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
1/2 cup Cottage Cheese
1 cup Heavy Cream
1/4 cup Cream Sherry
3 Large Eggs
1/3 cup Granulated Sugar
2 teaspoons Rosewater
1/2 teaspoon Nutmeg, Grated
1/4 teaspoon Cinnamon, Ground
1/4 cup Dried Currants
Crust:
Sift together the flour, salt, and sugar into a medium-sized mixing bowl. Add the butter to the flour all at once. Mix together until well blended. Then add the shortening and continue to blend by cutting it into the flour mixture. The mixture will begin to look like crumbs or small pebbles. It will have the texture of oatmeal. Sprinkle the water over the dough, distributing it evenly throughout. Gather it into a ball and wrap in plastic wrap. Chill for at least 30 minutes. Preheat oven to 450°F. When the pastry has chilled, roll out on a pastry board to a thickness of 1/8-inch. Roll the dough between two sheets of waxed paper. Roll dough up onto the rolling pin and transfer it to a 9-inch pie tin. Gently press into the tin without stretching. Use excess pastry for flutes or cut away with scissors. Prick bottom thoroughly to prevent trapped air from bubbling the dough. Butter a sheet of aluminum foil and place in shell. Weight down with beans. Bake crust for 7-10 minutes, then remove weight and foil. Return to oven and bake another 8-10 minutes until lightly browned.
Filling:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Press cottage cheese through sieve. Gently heat cream and sherry until steaming; do not scorch. Beat eggs, yolks, sugar, rosewater, spices in mixing bowl until frothy (about 5 minutes). Beat cottage cheese smooth, then add egg mixture and blend well. Add hot cream and sherry slowly to cheese mixture and beat until blended. Stir in currants and pour into prepared crust. Bake about 30 minutes, then allow to cool. Chill and sprinkle with cinnamon.
